name: Steak Teriyaki (and Chicken Teriyaki)
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Each dry sherry and reduced-sodium soy sauce
1/2 teaspoon Cornstarch dissolved in 1 teaspoon water
recipeInstructions:
1. In a glass or stainless-steel bowl, combine broth, sherry, soy sauce, sugar, ginger, and garlic. Stir to dissolve the sugar.
2. Add steak and turn several times to coat with marinade. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
3. Transfer steak to a rack in a broiling pan and set aside.
4. Pour marinade into a small saucepan and cook over high heat until the mixture comes to a boil. Stir in the dissolved cornstarch.
5. Reduce heat to medium and cook, stirring frequently, until the mixture is clear and thickened (about 5-10 minutes). Reduce heat to low and keep warm.
6. Broil steak 4-6 inches from the heat source for 4-5 minutes, turning the steak over and brushing with 2 tablespoons marinade. Broil for an additional 4-5 minutes or until done to taste.
7. Remove steak to a cutting board and cut into 1-inch thick strips. Arrange on a serving plate and top with remaining marinade.
NOTE: FOR CHICKEN TERIYAKI - Substitute chicken broth for beef broth and use skinned and boned chicken breasts instead of steak. Proceed according to the directions.
Makes 2 servings.
